//: Two independent packages, like DrummyFloyd/crunchy-userinit-controller:
- ".": the platform app — bumps images, the vX.Y.Z git tag ArgoCD
tracks (Application targetRevision), and Chart.yaml's appVersion
(default image tag). ArgoCD deploys straight from git, so this tag
is the actual deployable-state contract.
- "helm/waas": the chart's OWN SemVer (Chart.yaml `version`), bumped
only by changes under helm/, used solely for the OCI chart artifact
pushed for external `helm install --version` consumers. It can
legitimately diverge from the app version between chart releases —
that's normal Helm practice, not a bug.
The root package sets include-component-in-tag: false so its tag is
vX.Y.Z and NOT waas-vX.Y.Z (manifest mode prefixes with the
package-name by default). Only the git tag carries the 'v':
Chart.yaml's appVersion and the promoted image tags are bare X.Y.Z —
the ci.yml promote job derives VERSION=${TAG#v} for both the image
tags and the appVersion assertion. The chart package keeps its
component prefix (git tag chart-X.Y.Z, Release title chart: vX.Y.Z —
release-please's default <component>: v<version> titling) — no
collision, and promote-chart never compares its tag to a version
field.
